stem:
Mention the pass number for each of the following activities that occur in a two pass assembler:
A. Object code generation
B. Literals added to literal table
C. Listing printed
D. Address resolution of local symbols

Which of the following correctly assigns pass numbers?
reason: Options were parsed as the activity labels (A-D sub-items) rather than the actual answer choices (Pass 2/1 assignments). The stem should include all activities and the sub-question; options should be the four pass-number assignments.

stem:
A simple two-pass assembler does the following in the first pass:

(Select all that apply.)

options.text: It allocates space for the literals.
It computes the total length of the program.
It builds the symbol table for the symbols and their values.
It generates code for all the load and store register instructions.

reason: Option E text incorrectly included '(Select all that apply.)' which is part of the question framing, not the option text. Option E should only be 'None of the above.'
